evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Optimization, modernization, and end-to-end delivery discipline signal rigour; no velocity/speed language is present anywhere in the JD.
evidence.domain_risk: serving as a data partner to answer analytical questions and extract business insights
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Real estate/engineering MNC BI role affecting business decisions without financial-reporting or regulatory language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: leveraging Microsoft Azure services to modernize infrastructure
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: An existing SQL Server BI stack (SSIS/SSRS/Power BI) with a modernization initiative to Azure indicates a growing, established-but-evolving data practice.
evidence.jd_authorship: build paginated reports via SSRS, and develop data integration workflows through SSIS
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Power BI, DAX, Power Query, SSRS, SSIS, Azure) with precise application context make the day-to-day work very clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: serving as a data partner to answer analytical questions and extract business insights
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centers on internal BI delivery and being a data partner for analytical questions, placing it in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Candidates need proven BI development experience delivering end-to-end data projects
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs are execution-oriented (design, build, develop, write, serve) delivering scoped BI/ETL work rather than setting broader data strategy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No testing, data contracts, observability, or data quality language appears anywhere in the JD.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: None: the JD is framed entirely in delivery and modernization terms with no risk register.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: leveraging Microsoft Azure services to modernize infrastructure
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of designing new dashboards/semantic layers (greenfield) and modernizing/migrating existing SSIS/SSRS workloads to Azure (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
